selection.strategy: Seed each day, draw from different domain groups first, then mix in trap questions and shuffle the final set.
selection.note: Questions are raw concrete prompts so content can be edited without touching app logic.

questions.prompt: A $40 cat cushion is reduced by 25%. What is the new price?
questions.hint: 25% of 40 is 10, then subtract it.
questions.explanation: 25% of 40 is 10, so the new price is 40 - 10 = 30.

questions.prompt: A treat pouch has 2 salmon treats and 8 tuna treats. What is the probability of picking salmon?
questions.hint: Probability is wanted outcomes over total outcomes.
questions.explanation: There are 2 salmon treats out of 10 treats, so the probability is 1/5.

questions.prompt: A treat pouch has 2 salmon treats and 3 tuna treats. What is the probability of picking salmon?
questions.hint: Probability is wanted outcomes over total outcomes.
questions.explanation: There are 2 salmon treats out of 5 treats, so the probability is 2/5.

questions.prompt: A treat pouch has 2 salmon treats and 4 tuna treats. What is the probability of picking salmon?
questions.hint: Probability is wanted outcomes over total outcomes.
questions.explanation: There are 2 salmon treats out of 6 treats, so the probability is 1/3.

questions.prompt: A treat pouch has 2 salmon treats and 5 tuna treats. What is the probability of picking salmon?
questions.hint: Probability is wanted outcomes over total outcomes.
questions.explanation: There are 2 salmon treats out of 7 treats, so the probability is 2/7.

questions.prompt: A treat pouch has 2 salmon treats and 6 tuna treats. What is the probability of picking salmon?
questions.hint: Probability is wanted outcomes over total outcomes.
questions.explanation: There are 2 salmon treats out of 8 treats, so the probability is 1/4.

questions.prompt: A treat pouch has 2 salmon treats and 7 tuna treats. What is the probability of picking salmon?
questions.hint: Probability is wanted outcomes over total outcomes.
questions.explanation: There are 2 salmon treats out of 9 treats, so the probability is 2/9.

questions.hint: Divide both parts by 5 if you can.

questions.hint: Division by zero is not allowed.
questions.explanation: 5/0 is undefined because you cannot divide a number into zero equal groups.

questions.hint: This is a special case that is usually avoided at this level.
questions.explanation: 0^0 is usually avoided or called undefined/indeterminate in Year 7 maths. It needs more advanced context.
